name: Garden Growing Wild
paraphrase: Resist the temptation to manicure the garden into a formal, tidy arrangement; instead, let it grow half-wild, with self-seeded plants, native species and a tangle of growth that follows natural processes rather than rigid design. Alexander argues that an overly groomed garden is dead to the senses, while a garden allowed its own life invites birds, insects and quiet wonder, becoming a place where people actually want to spend time.
